pubmed_links: [Zufferey et al. (2012) A 600 kb deletion syndrome at 16p11.2 leads to energy imbalance and neuropsychiatric disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23054248/); [Kretz et al. (2023) Dissecting the autism-associated 16p11.2 locus identifies multiple drivers in neuroanatomical phenotypes and unveils a male-specific role for the major vault protein](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37968726/); [Ferreccio et al. (2024) TAOK2 drives opposing cilia length deficits in 16p11.2 deletion and duplication carriers](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39416068/)
summary: PPP4C lies within the recurrent 16p11.2 chromosomal region, copy-number variants of which are among the most frequent genetic causes of autism spectrum disorder and intellectual disability; mouse studies have identified Ppp4c as one of 13 genes driving neuroanatomical phenotypes (predominantly reduced brain structure volumes in males), and PPP4C has been shown to regulate primary cilia length in neural progenitor cells from 16p11.2 CNV carriers. (emerging evidence)
